data.content:
The file is generated successfully (`obj/Debug/net8.0/Generated/GeneratedInfo.cs`) but never added to the `Compile` item group, so Roslyn doesn't know about it.

**Fix:** Add the generated file to `@(Compile)` in the target:

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target generates the file but doesn't add it to the `Compile` item group. The target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"`, which means the file is generated before compilation starts, but the compiler doesn't know about it because it's not included in the `@(Compile)` items.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `Compile` item group within the target using:
```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
</ItemGroup>
```

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content: Build succeeds now. The problem was that the `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote the `.cs` file to disk but never added it to the `@(Compile)` item group. MSBuild's compiler only compiles files in that item group, so the generated file was ignored. Adding a `<Compile Include="..."/>` inside the target fixes it.
